expectations.text: internalEdges contains svc→core (workspace:* protocol) and web→core (file:../core link) — both recognized despite the different protocols
the svc→test-kit devDependencies edge is present and flagged as dev-only (it must not be presented as an ordinary runtime edge)
edges use layer names from the provided list (core, svc, web, test-kit), not the scoped package names (@edge/core etc.)
the legacy layer (no package.json) appears in gaps as having no recognizable manifest — not silently skipped, and no edges invented for it
expectations.evidence: legacy gaps=["legacy (packages/legacy) has no recognizable manifest (no package.json); internal and external dependencies for this layer cannot be mapped."], legacy edges=[]

expectations.text: externalDeps name the defining deps: fastify for svc, react for web, zod for core, vitest for test-kit
expectations.evidence: svc=[{"name":"fastify","role":"HTTP framework"},{"name":"typescript","role":"build tool (tsc)","devOnly":true}], web=[{"name":"react","role":"UI framework"},{"name":"react-dom","role":"UI framework (DOM renderer)"},{"name":"vite","role":"build tool","devOnly":true}], core=[{"name":"zod","role":"schema library"}], test-kit=[{"name":"vitest","role":"test framework"}]

expectations.text: ordering identifies core as the provider consumed by svc and web (changes first); test-kit's provider role, if mentioned, is qualified as dev-only
expectations.evidence: ordering={"providers":["core"],"consumers":["svc","web"],"signal":"core is the sole runtime provider, consumed by both svc and web; when a task spans layers, change core first, then svc and web in either order. test-kit is consumed only as a devDependency of svc, so it carries little ordering weight. legacy participates in no internal edges.","suggestedDispatchOrder":["core","svc","web"]}
